channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: Ontario education minister says he can't interfere in Catholic board's Pride flag ban
Here’s how much Ontario taxpayers are on the hook for in private jet purchase and sale
item.description: Ontario taxpayers are on the hook for nearly $200,000 over Premier Doug Ford’s flip-flop on a $28.9-million private jet purchase, newly released documents from the premier’s office show.

item.title: Ontario inspecting commercial truck driver colleges after scathing audit: minister
item.description: All Ontario career colleges that offer commercial truck driver training will have been inspected by the end of next month, the province’s minister of colleges and universities said Wednesday.

item.title: Toronto MP Erskine-Smith says provincial leadership bid ‘less likely’ now as he challenges nomination loss
item.description: Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith cast doubt Tuesday on a potential leadership bid for the provincial Liberals, hours after filing a notice of appeal to that party challenging the result of a nomination race that he lost.

item.title: Commercial trucker training and licensing problematic in Ontario: auditor general
item.description: Ontario is not effectively monitoring commercial truck driver training and licensing regimes, leading to many unqualified drivers on the roads, the province’s auditor general found in a special report released Tuesday.

item.title: Doug Ford says he won’t attack other bids in his push for Toronto as defence bank HQ
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford on Tuesday defended Toronto’s bid to host a multinational defence bank, saying he did not believe in attacking rival campaigns by other Canadian cities.

item.title: Some Ontario students are being sent home because schools can’t meet their special needs: auditor
item.description: Students with special educational needs in three Ontario schools boards, including the TCDSB, are facing long wait times for help and sometimes being sent home because schools are unable to meet their needs, a new report from Ontario’s auditor general reveals.

item.title: Ontario AI used to take notes in medical appointments ‘hallucinated’ in testing: auditor
item.description: A wide-ranging report by Ontario’s Auditor General has found that the province’s artificial intelligence (AI) strategy still lacks several “key components” and there are problems with thousands of civil servants using unsafe AI websites, and inaccuracies in AI notetaking used by some family doctors and other healthcare professionals.

item.title: Ontario planning to crack down on ticket reselling for large events such as FIFA World Cup
item.description: Ontario is reallocating resources to help crack down on unfair ticket reselling ahead of the FIFA World Cup games in Toronto.

item.title: Ontario Liberal interim leader says nomination was fair, despite candidate's comments
item.description: The interim leader of the Ontario Liberal Party says a nomination race this weekend to select a byelection candidate was fair, open and transparent, despite suspicion being raised by Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith.

item.title: Wait times at Ontario emergency rooms have spiked over past 5 years, study says
item.description: Wait times for people seeking care at emergency departments across Ontario have dramatically increased over the past five years amid a “deepening Ontario hospital funding crisis,” according to a new report.

item.title: Ontario nurses launch constitutional challenge over lack of right to strike
item.description: The Ontario Nurses’ Association is launching a constitutional challenge of a law that prevents them from taking any form of job action during the bargaining process.

item.title: Ontario pharmacists now allowed to treat these 9 conditions
item.description: Ontario pharmacists will soon be allowed to administer more vaccines and treat more common ailments, as part of the government’s continuing moves to expand their scope of practice.

item.title: Liberal MP Erskine-Smith loses nomination battle for Ontario provincial byelection
item.description: Liberal MP Nate Erskine-Smith has lost a provincial nomination race, failing to clear the first of several hurdles toward the leadership of the Ontario Liberals.

item.title: Ontario Liberal MPP from Etobicoke enters leadership race
item.description: Ontario Liberal legislator Lee Fairclough has announced her bid for the party’s leadership.
